drugs.name: Aspirin
drugs.description: Used to reduce pain, fever, or inflammation and as a blood thinner.
drugs.indication: Pain relief, fever reduction, and cardiovascular protection.
drugs.boxed_warning: Reye's syndrome in children; increased risk of serious GI bleeding.

drugs.name: Warfarin
drugs.description: Blood thinner used to prevent blood clots.
drugs.indication: Prophylaxis and treatment of venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism.
drugs.boxed_warning: Fatal or nonfatal bleeding; regular INR monitoring is mandatory.

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.description: Used for pain relief and reducing inflammation.
drugs.indication: Mild to moderate pain, inflammation, and fever.
drugs.boxed_warning: Cardiovascular thrombotic events; serious gastrointestinal bleeding and ulceration.

drugs.name: Lisinopril
drugs.description: Used to treat high blood pressure and heart failure.
drugs.indication: Hypertension, heart failure, and post-myocardial infarction.
drugs.boxed_warning: Fetal toxicity; discontinue as soon as pregnancy is detected.

drugs.name: Simvastatin
drugs.description: Used to lower cholesterol and prevent cardiovascular disease.
drugs.indication: Hyperlipidemia and reduction of cardiovascular risk.
drugs.boxed_warning: Myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk, especially at high doses or with interacting drugs.

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.description: First-line medication for the treatment of type 2 diabetes.
drugs.boxed_warning: Lactic acidosis; risk increases with renal impairment and sepsis.

drugs.name: Spironolactone
drugs.description: Used to treat fluid build-up and high blood pressure.
drugs.boxed_warning: Tumorigenic potential in chronic animal studies.

drugs.name: Digoxin
drugs.description: Used to treat heart failure and certain heart rhythm problems.
drugs.indication: Congestive heart failure and atrial fibrillation.
drugs.boxed_warning: Digitalis toxicity risk; monitor serum levels and electrolytes.

drugs.name: Amiodarone
drugs.description: Used to treat and prevent certain types of serious, life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias.
drugs.indication: Ventricular fibrillation and unstable ventricular tachycardia.
drugs.boxed_warning: Pulmonary toxicity, hepatotoxicity, and proarrhythmic effects.

drugs.name: Paxlovid
drugs.description: Treatment of mild-to-moderate COVID-19 in adults and pediatric patients.
drugs.indication: SARS-CoV-2 infection treatment in high-risk patients.
drugs.boxed_warning: Significant drug-drug interaction potential via CYP3A inhibition.

drugs.name: Sildenafil
drugs.description: Used for erectile dysfunction and pulmonary arterial hypertension.
drugs.boxed_warning: Co-administration with organic nitrates can cause life-threatening hypotension.

drugs.name: Nitroglycerin
drugs.description: Used to treat or prevent angina (chest pain).
drugs.boxed_warning: Do not use with PDE5 inhibitors (e.g., Sildenafil) due to risk of fatal blood pressure drop.

interactions.description: Critical bleeding risk. Aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation while Warfarin anticoagulates, creating a dangerous synergy.
interactions.clinical_action: Avoid co-administration. If necessary, switch to a safer alternative or monitor INR daily.

interactions.description: Life-threatening hypotension. Nitrates and PDE5 inhibitors both increase cGMP, causing profound vasodilation.
interactions.mechanism: Synergistic increase in cyclic GMP levels.
interactions.clinical_action: Fatal interaction. Do not use Sildenafil within 24 hours of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.description: Significantly increases bleeding risk. NSAIDs also cause GI mucosal injury.
interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic synergism and GI mucosal injury.
interactions.clinical_action: Avoid Ibuprofen; use Acetaminophen for pain relief if patient is on Warfarin.

interactions.description: Risk of life-threatening hyperkalemia (dangerously high potassium).
interactions.clinical_action: Monitor serum potassium levels weekly and adjust dosages accordingly.

interactions.description: Increased risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis (muscle toxicity).
interactions.mechanism: Inhibition of CYP3A4-mediated metabolism of Simvastatin by Amiodarone.
interactions.clinical_action: Limit Simvastatin dose to 20mg daily or switch to a non-CYP3A4 metabolized statin.

interactions.description: Paxlovid contains Ritonavir, which massively increases Simvastatin levels.
interactions.mechanism: Strong CYP3A4 inhibition by Ritonavir component.
interactions.clinical_action: Discontinue Simvastatin 12 hours before starting Paxlovid; resume 5 days after completion.

interactions.description: Spironolactone may decrease renal clearance of Digoxin.
interactions.clinical_action: Monitor Digoxin serum concentrations and adjust dose if needed.
